
```markdown
---
title: "Serie A: From Seven Sisters to Juve's Nine"
subtitle: "Italian football's collapse and partial recovery"
author: "Eric Schnitger"
date: 2026-04-27
categories: [data-visualization, python, sports-analytics, web-scraping]
image: thumbnail.png
description: |
 An interactive analysis of Serie A from 1992 to 2025, tracking the
 decline of the "Seven Sisters" through Juventus's nine consecutive
 titles and the recent resurgence of Inter and Napoli.
format:
 html:
   code-fold: true
   code-summary: "Show code"
   code-tools: true
   toc: true
   toc-depth: 3
execute:
 echo: true
 warning: false
 freeze: auto
---

## The Question

In the 1990s, Serie A was the world's strongest league. Seven clubs competed
for titles. Then came financial collapse, Calciopoli, and Juventus winning nine
in a row. Has Italy recovered?

## Key Findings

::: {.callout-tip}
## Summary

- **The Seven Sisters era (1992-2006)** saw 5 different champions
- **Calciopoli (2006)** broke the old power structure
- **Juventus won 9 consecutive titles (2012-2020)**, pushing winning points from ~75 to 90+
- **Post-Juve**: Inter (2021, 2024) and Napoli (2023) won, but the gap to the bottom remains large
- **Multiple bankruptcies** (Parma, Fiorentina) distinguish Serie A from other leagues
:::

```{python}
#| label: load-data-serie-a
#| code-summary: "Import and load data"

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "..")

from _utils import (
   LEAGUES, scrape_league, enrich_dataframe,
   build_league_chart, build_frequency_tables,
)
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

config = LEAGUES["serie_a"]
df = scrape_league("serie_a", cache_dir=".")
df = enrich_dataframe(df)

print(f"Dataset: {len(df)} seasons loaded")
df[["Season", "Champion", "Title-Winning Points", "Survived Relegation",
   "Relegation Survival Points", "Relegated 1", "Relegated 2", "Relegated 3"]].tail(10)
```

## Interactive Analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-main-serie-a
#| fig-cap: "Serie A: Title-winning vs. relegation survival points"
#| code-summary: "Build visualization"

eras = [
   {"label": "Full History (1992-2025)", "start": df["Season"].iloc[0],
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Serie A: Seven Sisters to Monopoly (1992-2025)"},
   {"label": "Seven Sisters (1992-2006)", "start": "1992/93", "end": "2005/06",
    "title": "Serie A: The Seven Sisters (1992-2006)"},
   {"label": "Post-Calciopoli (2006-2012)", "start": "2006/07", "end": "2011/12",
    "title": "Serie A: Post-Calciopoli (2006-2012)"},
   {"label": "Juve Dynasty (2012-2020)", "start": "2011/12", "end": "2019/20",
    "title": "Serie A: Juventus's Nine (2012-2020)"},
   {"label": "Post-Juve (2020-2025)", "start": "2020/21",
    "end": df["Season"].iloc[-1],
    "title": "Serie A: Post-Juve (2020-2025)"},
]

outliers = []
notable = [
   ("2013/14", "Juve 102 pts", "#FFD54F", 0, -25),
   ("2022/23", "Napoli dominant", "#26C6DA", -50, -30),
]
for season, text, color, ax, ay in notable:
   match = df[df["Season"] == season]
   if not match.empty:
       outliers.append({
           "season": season,
           "y": int(match["Title-Winning Points"].iloc[0]),
           "text": text,
           "color": color,
           "ax": ax,
           "ay": ay,
       })

fig = build_league_chart(df, config, eras=eras, outliers=outliers)
fig.show()
```

## Champions League Concentration

```{python}
#| label: tbl-top4
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequent Serie A Top 4 finishers"
#| code-summary: "Top 4 frequency"

top4_clubs, relegated_clubs = build_frequency_tables(df, config)
top4_clubs.head(10).style.format({"Percentage": "{:.1f}%"}).hide(axis="index")
```

## Relegated Clubs

```{python}
#| label: tbl-relegated
#| tbl-cap: "Most frequently relegated Serie A clubs"
#| code-summary: "Relegation frequency"

relegated_clubs.head(10).style.hide(axis="index")
```

## Context

- **18 teams until 2004/05**, then expanded to 20
- **Calciopoli (2006)**: match-fixing scandal, Juventus relegated and stripped of titles
- **Multiple bankruptcies**: Parma, Fiorentina restarted from lower divisions
- **"2 points for a win"** used until 1994/95 (Wikipedia tables use retroactive 3-point recalculation)

---

*Part of [The European Football Divide](../) project.*
```
